items.caption:
🍋Keep Flies Away with Lemons & Cloves 🍋
Cut a lemon in half and press whole cloves into it before setting it on your picnic table or wherever you are eating outside. The strong scent of clove oil (especially eugenol) helps repel flies, while the citrus adds another fragrance they tend to avoid. Follow along for more grandma hacks!
items.transcript: Want to keep those annoying flies away from your food? Just place some lemon and cloves around your food. The aroma of the cloves and the citrus oil in the lemon act as a natural fly repellent.

items.caption:
Part 2: Comment “Prep” and I’ll DM you all the recipes for the food I prepped and the make-ahead meals that helped feed our family vacation of 19. Part 1 was: Everything I made on vacation for our family of 19! 
Every year, our family rents one big vacation house and spends a week together. There are sandy feet, grandchildren climbing into bed for morning snuggles, late-night card games, and memories that somehow become family stories before the week is even over.  But feeding 19  people for a week doesn’t happen by accident.
Over the years, I’ve learned that a little planning before we leave makes all the difference once we arrive. My goal isn’t to spend vacation cooking.  It’s to make sure everyone can relax, eat well, and enjoy themselves without constant grocery runs, takeout, or wondering what’s for lunch.
Before we leave, I make everything I can ahead of time. Anything that travels well, reheats beautifully, or simply makes life easier gets checked off my list at home. Once we’re there, every family takes a turn making dinner, which has become one of our favorite vacation traditions.
Comment “Prep” below, and I’ll send you all of the recipes I made ahead of time.
items.transcript: Here's everything I prepped for our one-week family vacation for 19 people. You heard that right, 19. But you know what? If you feed them, they will come. Over the years, I've learned though that getting as much done ahead of time is non-negotiable. Make-ahead breakfast, snacks the kids love, and of course my famous chocolate chip cookies. After so many vacations, I've developed a bit of a refridge stocking strategy. We have a deli basket, plenty of prepared salads, washed fruits and vegetables, all of the breakfasts I make ahead, and of course a section of the refridge dedicated entirely to golden hour. Having breakfast made in advance is one of my favorite vacation hacks. I made one giant breakfast crunch wrap, brought it up whole. We just reheated it when we were ready to eat. This year, I transformed my classic blueberry buckle into a countertop blueberry buckle cake, and I don't think I'm ever going back. It made the perfect vacation breakfast or snack, but you know, usually both. Of course, no family vacation would be complete without my famous chocolate chip cookies. I made the dough ahead of time and baked fresh cookies off whenever the craving struck. Of course, snacks are a must on vacation. I came prepared with my s'mores granola. It has become something of a classic in our house, and my granddaughter even pitched in to help. We enjoyed it for breakfast over yogurt and sprinkled over ice cream. The honey buffalo pretzels are a little spicy with a bit of a kick. We had a wonderful week, and guess what? No one went hungry, that's for sure.

items.caption:
Comment “Vacation Menu” and I’ll DM you the recipes for everything we made on our family vacation. With 19 people under one roof, someone is always hungry. There are early risers looking for breakfast, kids asking for snacks all afternoon, and somehow everyone is ready for dinner by six o’clock. The secret isn’t making fancy meals, it’s keeping things simple. Our favorite vacation meals are the ones that bring everyone together without keeping anyone stuck in the kitchen. We rely on fresh ingredients, family favorites, easy grilling recipes, make-ahead staples, and plenty of flexibility.
So excited to share everything we cooked, grilled, snacked on, and sipped during our vacation. If you’re feeding a crowd this summer or just looking for easy meal ideas that leave more time for making memories, I hope you’ll find plenty of inspiration. Comment “Vacation Menu” below, and l’ll send you the recipes we made during the week!
items.transcript: Here's everything I made during our vacation for our family of 19. During these summer trips, of course, I used the grill as much as possible. First up, pasta alla bangole, just lovely simple ingredients that come together for the most delicious dinner al fresco. I started making what my kids call beach bread years ago, which is simply an antipasto salad stuffed in a crispy baguette. We use a dedicated small wastebasket to keep our beach bread dry when going to the beach because no one likes a soggy sandwich. To quickly chill the cooler, Mr. Babs simply adds salt. Each year I try to make a signature drink. This year's drink was It's That Time peach iced tea. It was a huge hit with all the adults. You just keep adding iced tea as the day goes on, so it's a drink that can literally last all day. The grill was in such rough shape that we checked with the owner. Mr. Babs made a quick trip to the local hardware store to replace the grates and flavor bars for a little over $50. One day we wanted to enjoy hot sausage and broth sandwiches, so we just added hot bricks to the cooler to keep the food warm at the beach. One of my favorite meals of the trip was this grilled pesto shrimp and Caesar salad. The romaine is lightly charred while still maintaining the crunch, and of course what would grilled Caesar be without garlic bread? My new hack is oiling the grates with beef tallow. The food doesn't stick and taste even better, and grilled shrimp is always a huge hit with our crowd. I wasn't sure about the viral hack of melting good chocolate in the sun for dipping strawberries, but I'm a believer. Talk about easy and delicious. Another great meal we enjoyed was my grilled chicken kebabs with white barbecue sauce, along with my no-cook formerly known as sun tomatoes that now cook in the refridge. It's a deliciously fresh sauce, ready to be served on all kind of pasta. The chicken is marinated in a rub before grilling and served with a barbecue sauce, which we end up using all week long on sandwiches. And of course, don't forget that gorgeous local corn. Last but not least, the grandkids wanted this viral pickle dip. Well, I ended up making it twice. That's how good it was. The best part, you store it right in the pickle container so it can go anywhere.

items.caption: The Basket House is all ready to celebrate and so are we!! Headed to lead the fife and drum corps in colonial garb. Happy 4th of July!! God Bless America 🇺🇸

musicInfo.artist_name: United States Army Old Guard Fife and Drum Corps

items.caption:
After 40 years of family vacations, we have learned that the best trips aren’t the ones that go perfectly but the ones where everyone pitches in, rolls with the unexpected, and makes room for a little fun along the way.

There will be piles of towels, dishes in the sink, and the occasional debate over sleeping arrangements. But years from now, no one will remember who took out the trash or who got the biggest bedroom. They’ll remember the late-night card games, the belly laughs, the inside jokes, and the traditions that become family stories.

Family vacations aren’t about perfection. They’re about being together. And those memories are worth every bit of planning, flexibility, and teamwork it takes to make them happen. ❤️
items.transcript: After 40 years of family vacations, we finally figured out what actually works. First, don't fight over bedrooms. The bedroom drama sometimes lasts longer than the vacation. After the parents or grandparents make their selection, everybody's name gets thrown into a hat and we just go from there. You get what you get and you don't get upset. The best way to bond is to have some family games. Get out and play croquet, play volleyball, whatever your family does. And at night, have some board games to bring everyone together. No one wants to cook every night. I know you're going to go out for dinner a couple of nights, but then everyone takes a night. They're responsible for the dinner for the family. And if you're not cooking that night, you're on KP duty, kitchen patrol. Be a team player. If the garbage needs to go out, take it out. We have a saying on vacation. If you spot it, you got it. Be flexible. Not everything's going to go as planned. And also, respect people's downtime. Maybe the mornings just curl up with a book or take a nap. Allow them to do that. Hey, we're on vacation. Relax and enjoy yourself. Trying new activities helps families bond. And actually, it creates a lot of memories too. So go on vacation, relax, and enjoy the ones you love the most in the world.

items.caption:
Some of the best summertime tricks have been passed down for generations. Here are a few old-fashioned favorites that are still worth knowing.

🍋 Keep Flies Away with Lemons & Cloves
Cut a lemon in half and press whole cloves into it before setting it on your picnic table or wherever you are eating outside. The strong scent of clove oil (especially eugenol) helps repel flies, while the citrus adds another fragrance they tend to avoid.

🧺 Cover Food with a Damp Tea Towel
Before plastic wrap, Grandma covered dishes with a clean, damp tea towel. The moisture weighs the towel down and helps create a cool barrier that keeps flying insects out while still keeping food moist. 
 
🫖 Take a Tea Bath for Sunburn
Steep 8-10 black tea bags, let the tea cool, and add it to a cool bath for a soothing soak. Black tea contains natural tannins and polyphenols that help calm irritated skin and take the “sting” out.

🥄 The Hot Spoon Trick for Bug Bites
Place a spoon in hot water for a few minutes, then gently press it against a mosquito bite for a few seconds. The warmth temporarily reduces the itch by affecting the proteins involved in your body’s itch response.

🐝 Set Out a Can of Tuna for Yellow Jackets
Place an open can of tuna 30–50 feet away from where everyone is eating. Yellow jackets are attracted to the protein and will head there instead of your picnic table, giving you a little more peace while you enjoy your meal.

🧱 Use Hot Bricks to Keep Food Warm
Wrap bricks in foil, heat them on the grill, then place them in the bottom of a newspaper-lined cooler before adding your hot dishes. The bricks act as thermal mass, slowly releasing stored heat to help keep food warm for hours.
items.transcript: Did your grandmother ever teach you these summer hacks? Want to take the sting out of that sunburn? Just steep 8 to 10 black tea bags, set them steep for 15 minutes, pour it into a cold bath. And throw those tea bags in too. The tannins in the black tea will help reduce inflammation and ease the burning sensation. Want to keep your food warm at the beach all day long? Just heat up some bricks and pop them in your cooler. Newspaper below and on top. Place your food. You're ready to go. Want to keep the bugs off your food at your next barbecue? Dampen a tea towel and just cover away. Dampening the towel will keep it in place and also help keep your food moist. Want to keep those annoying flies away from your food? Just place some lemon and cloves around your food. The aroma of the cloves and the citrus oil in the lemon act as a natural fly repellent. Want to take the itch out of those bug bites? Just warm a spoon, place it right on that bite, and it'll take the itchies away immediately. Want to keep those yellow jackets away from ruining your next party? Just open up a can of tuna and water, place it 30 feet from the food, and let them go to this. They're looking for protein and this is where they're going to find it.

items.caption:
#QVCPartner Shopping has changed over the years, but one thing hasn’t. People still want honest recommendations from people they trust.

It was such a pleasure celebrating @QVC’s 40th Anniversary and seeing firsthand how they’re continuing to bring people together through live shopping, real conversations, and quality products.

Thank you, QVC, for including me in such a special day. Here’s to 40 years of Quality, Value, and Convenience, and to what’s next! ❤️ And make sure to keep shopping #QVCTikTokSuperBrandDay in their TikTok shop until 7/2. #LoveQVC
items.transcript: Come spend the day with me as I headed into New York City to celebrate QVC's 40th anniversary for a day filled with conversation, inspiration, and a look at where live shopping is headed next. I had the opportunity to join a panel discussion, connect with fellow creators, and share my thoughts on the future of live shopping. One of my favorite moments was joining QVC's TikTok Superbrand Day where I got to share some of the products that I love and connect with shoppers in real time. We also explored new products and had a little fun along the way. And wow, John can really dance. Happy 40th anniversary, QVC!

items.caption:
One thing I’ve learned over the years is that entertaining is easier when you have pieces that do the heavy lifting for you.

This summer, I’ve been reaching for some of my favorite Birch Lane entertaining essentials again and again. The outdoor bar cart keeps everything organized, the melamine dinner and salad plates make outdoor dining easy, and the serving bowl is perfect for sharing family favorites. I added a table lamp for a warm glow as the sun started to set, served homemade lemonade in my pitcher and glasses, and ended the evening gathered around the firepit.

I love that @birchlane pieces are timeless, practical, and meant to be used. Their classic style for joyful living fits right in with the way I love to entertain—creating welcoming spaces where family and friends can gather, relax, and make memories together.

And because every gathering deserves a sweet treat, I made mini S’mores Dot Cakes in mason jars.

Mini S’mores Dot Cakes

Ingredients:
• 2 ½ cups chocolate chips
• 2 cups heavy cream
• 16 oz Cool Whip, thawed
• 7.5 oz marshmallow fluff
• 2 cups mini marshmallows
• Graham cracker crumbs
• Rainbow sprinkles

Directions:
Heat the heavy cream until simmering, then pour it over the chocolate chips. Stir until smooth to make a ganache and let cool slightly.

In a separate bowl, fold together the Cool Whip, marshmallow fluff, and mini marshmallows.

In mini mason jars, add one layer of chocolate ganache, one layer of graham cracker crumbs, and one layer of the marshmallow mixture. Dip the tops into rainbow sprinkles and refrigerate until chilled.

Simple recipes, beautiful entertaining pieces, and people gathered around the table. That’s summer. 💛

#mybirchlane #birchlanepartner
items.transcript: It was the last week of school, and we decided to celebrate at the basket house. We picked fresh mint for lemonade, ordered pizza, and the boys even had a chance to fish. Ford caught the absolute biggest fish we've ever seen come out of the river. I put my own twist on that viral dot cake trend with these dot s'mores cups for everyone to enjoy. As the sun started to set, we made traditional s'mores too. That's the beauty of summer entertaining. It doesn't have to be complicated. Just good food and fresh air with the people that you love most.
